message:
fix(lock): use platform-specific process detection for macOS compatibility

The previous implementation used /proc filesystem to check if a process
is running, but /proc doesn't exist on macOS.

- Linux: Use /proc/{pid} which is efficient and available
- macOS: Use `ps -p {pid}` command which works reliably
- Windows: Conservative approach (assume not running)

Fixes test failures on macOS: test_is_process_running_with_pid_one,
test_lock_acquire_and_release, test_lock_file_contains_pid, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
